articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

articles.description: Borussia Dortmund captain Emre Can will miss the rest of the season with a torn ACL in his left knee.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Exequiel Palacios (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Odilon Kossounou (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
Deniz Undav (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card.
First Half ends, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 0, VfB Stuttgart 0.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Piero Hincapié replaces Jonathan Tah.

Robert Andrich (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1, VfB Stuttgart 2. Amine Adli (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Álex Grimaldo.
Enzo Millot (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Florian Wirtz replaces Jonas Hofmann.

Maximilian Mittelstädt (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Anthony Rouault replaces Chris Führich.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Silas Katompa Mvumpa replaces Leonidas Stergiou.
Piero Hincapié (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Nathan Tella replaces Jeremie Frimpong.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Josip Stanisic replaces Álex Grimaldo.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Pascal Stenzel replaces Jamie Leweling.

Goal!  Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, VfB Stuttgart 2. Robert Andrich (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Piero Hincapié   following a set piece situation.
Second Half ends, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, VfB Stuttgart 2.

article.headline: Leverkusen leave it late again to maintain record unbeaten run
article.description: Bundesliga champions Bayer Leverkusen scored a late equaliser to come from two goals down and rescue a 2-2 draw against VfB Stuttgart on Saturday.
article.linkText: Leverkusen leave it late to maintain unbeaten run

article.story:
Bundesliga champions Bayer Leverkusen scored a last-gasp equaliser through Robert Andrich to come from two goals down and rescue a 2-2 draw against VfB Stuttgart on Saturday that extended their record unbeaten run to 46 matches across all competitions.

The Germany international slotted home after Florian Wirtz had floated a free kick into the box, to complete their latest comeback after also scoring a stoppage-time goal to snatch a 1-1 draw against Borussia Dortmund last week.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Xabi Alonso's team, who play Roma in the Europa League semifinals and are also through to the DFB-Pokal final, have already secured their first ever league title.

They have also come from behind on numerous occasions this season and have now scored 17 goals in the 89th minute or later in all competitions.

After a low-key first half Leverkusen were quickly 2-0 behind after the break with Chris Führich drilling in on the rebound after keeper Lukas Hradecky had palmed a shot on to the post in the 47th minute.

Deniz Undav doubled the visitors' lead 10 minutes later and only after Stuttgart came close to a third goal did Leverkusen launch their comeback.

They cut the deficit with Adli's low drive in the 61st minute that sailed past keeper Alexander Nübel, who then made three sensational saves to protect Stuttgart's slim lead.

Leverkusen's Hradecky responded with a fine save of his own to stop Stuttgart's Serhou Guirassy in the 87th before Andrich came to the rescue deep in stoppage time to extend their impressive undefeated run.

Stuttgart are third on 64, five behind second-placed Bayern Munich. Leverkusen are top on 81 points with three matches remaining.
